description_long: This object is a robust war hammer or pickaxe featuring a long, straight wooden handle with a dark, textured grip at one end for secure handling. The head is forged from metal and consists of a blunt hammer face on one side and a sharply curved, pointed pick on the opposite side, making it suitable for both striking and piercing tasks. The metal head is detailed with angular facets and a central ridge, giving it a reinforced, medieval appearance. The handle appears reinforced with metal bands or inlays along its length, suggesting durability and strength. The overall design is utilitarian yet ornate, with a focus on both function and visual impact. This tool would be at home in urban settings such as construction sites, garages, or as a decorative or functional piece in themed environments.
description: A heavy-duty war hammer or pickaxe with a long wooden handle, dark grip, and a metal head featuring both a blunt hammer and a pointed pick. The design is reinforced and visually striking, blending utility with medieval aesthetics.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the handle, the hammer face, and the prominent curved pick.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the pointed pick and the narrow profile of the head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle and the hammer face, with the pick curving away.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the hammer face and the alignment of the handle behind it.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a dark, textured leather grip; cylindrical cross-section; reinforced with metal bands; designed for secure two-handed use.
attribute_blade_design: Dual-function head with a blunt hammer face and a sharply curved, pointed pick; angular facets and central ridge; no beard; robust and reinforced.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and possibly pinned mounting with visible metal reinforcement at the head-handle junction.
